original_hypothesis.title: isSynced Check in DelayBuffer Can Cause Underflow Revert After Chain Fork
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The isSynced function computes block.number - self.prevBlockNumber without safe subtraction. Under Solidity 0.8's checked arithmetic, if prevBlockNumber > block.number (possible after a chain fork where block numbers reset or if a forked chain has lower block numbers), this would revert with an underflow. Since isSynced is called by isDelayProofRequired (which gates batch posting) and isUpdatable (which gates buffer updates), an underflow would brick all batch posting functions that check delay proof requirements.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: removeDelayAfterFork resets delayBlocks/futureBlocks/delaySeconds/futureSeconds to 1 but does NOT reset buffer state (prevBlockNumber, prevSequencedBlockNumber). If a fork results in lower block numbers on the new chain, the buffer's prevBlockNumber could exceed block.number, causing isSynced to revert and bricking delay-bufferable batch posting.

proof_of_signal.pattern: return block.number - self.prevBlockNumber <= self.threshold; — unchecked subtraction. removeDelayAfterFork at SequencerInbox.sol:196-200 resets delay params but NOT buffer.prevBlockNumber.

preconditions: New chain has block.number < buffer.prevBlockNumber from old chain
removeDelayAfterFork is called (resets delays but not buffer)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: block.number - self.prevBlockNumber underflows when block.number < prevBlockNumber
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: After fork, any batch posting with delay proof check reverts permanently
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit; complete denial of service for batch posting on forked chain

economic_questions: In a fork scenario, how likely is block.number to be lower on the new chain?
Can the admin fix this by calling setBufferConfig to reset buffer state?
Does this permanently brick the delay-bufferable sequencer inbox?
validation_requirements: Simulate fork with lower block numbers and test isSynced
Verify removeDelayAfterFork doesn't reset buffer state
Check if setBufferConfig can recover from this state

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete batch posting failure on delay-bufferable chains

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All L2 users on the forked chain
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of L2 functionality until resolved

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: buffer.prevBlockNumber values, fork scenario block numbers

validation_result.mechanism: Unchecked subtraction underflow in isSynced after fork
validation_result.impact_summary: After a chain fork that results in lower block numbers, isSynced() reverts due to underflow, bricking all delay-bufferable batch posting functions until admin calls setBufferConfig.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The isSynced function at DelayBuffer.sol:97-99 performs `block.number - self.prevBlockNumber` using Solidity 0.8's checked arithmetic. If prevBlockNumber exceeds block.number, this reverts with an arithmetic underflow. The removeDelayAfterFork function at SequencerInbox.sol:196-200 only resets delayBlocks/futureBlocks/delaySeconds/futureSeconds to 1, but does NOT reset buffer.prevBlockNumber or buffer.prevSequencedBlockNumber. This is an inconsistency because the sibling function calcBuffer in DelayBuffer.sol:47-48 handles this case gracefully with `end > start ? end - start : 0`. The code clearly anticipates fork scenarios (removeDelayAfterFork exists specifically for this), but the buffer state reset is incomplete. Since isSynced is called by isDelayProofRequired (which gates regular batch posting) and isUpdatable (which gates delay proof batch posting), all batch posting variants would revert. ForceInclusion would still work since it calls buffer.update() which goes through calcPendingBuffer/calcBuffer which handles the case safely. Admin recovery is possible via setBufferConfig which calls buffer.update(uint64(block.number)) through calcPendingBuffer which also handles the case safely.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly visible in the code. isSynced (line 97-99) uses bare subtraction while calcBuffer (line 47-48) uses safe comparison. removeDelayAfterFork (SequencerInbox.sol:196-200) resets delay params but not buffer state. The code pattern inconsistency is the root cause.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Uses Solidity 0.8 checked subtraction. If self.prevBlockNumber > block.number (possible after chain fork), this reverts instead of returning false. Compare with calcBuffer which uses 'end > start ? end - start : 0' pattern.

Resets delay parameters but does NOT reset buffer.prevBlockNumber or buffer.prevSequencedBlockNumber. After a fork with potentially different block numbers, the stale buffer state can cause isSynced to underflow.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
uint256 elapsed = end > start ? end - start : 0;
uint256 delay = sequenced > start ? sequenced - start : 0;
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This function correctly handles the case where end < start by returning 0. The isSynced function does not follow this same safe pattern, creating an inconsistency.

calling_sequence: 2. removeDelayAfterFork() called - resets delay params but NOT buffer.prevBlockNumber
3. Batch poster calls addSequencerL2BatchFromOrigin with new delayed messages
6. block.number - self.prevBlockNumber underflows if prevBlockNumber > block.number
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path goes: addSequencerL2BatchFromOrigin -> isDelayProofRequired (SequencerInbox.sol) -> buffer.isSynced() (DelayBuffer.sol:97-99) -> underflow revert. The delay proof variants also fail: addSequencerL2BatchFromBlobsDelayProof -> delayProofImpl -> buffer.isUpdatable() -> isSynced() -> revert. ForceInclusion works because it calls buffer.update() which goes through calcPendingBuffer -> calcBuffer which has safe comparison.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that are relevant to this specific issue. The vulnerability exists in the contract logic regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario requires: (1) isDelayBufferable is true, (2) a chain fork occurs changing block.chainid, (3) the new chain has block.number < buffer.prevBlockNumber, (4) removeDelayAfterFork is called. The most critical question is whether block.number can be lower after a fork. On standard Ethereum/Arbitrum chains, forks continue from the same block number, making the trigger unlikely. However, on custom L2/L3 chains or in theoretical fork scenarios with block number resets, this IS possible. The code defect exists regardless - isSynced uses unsafe subtraction while calcBuffer uses safe comparison for the same type of calculation.
validation_result.impact_assessment: If triggered: all delay-bufferable batch posting functions revert (both regular and delay-proof variants). ForceInclusion still works. Admin can recover by calling setBufferConfig which resets prevBlockNumber via buffer.update(). Impact duration is from fork until admin intervention. The severity is MEDIUM because: (1) it requires an unusual fork scenario, (2) forceInclusion provides a fallback, (3) admin recovery is available.
recommended_mitigations: Change isSynced to use safe comparison: `return block.number >= self.prevBlockNumber && block.number - self.prevBlockNumber <= self.threshold;` or `return (block.number > self.prevBlockNumber ? block.number - self.prevBlockNumber : 0) <= self.threshold;`
Add buffer state reset to removeDelayAfterFork: `if (isDelayBufferable) { buffer.prevBlockNumber = uint64(block.number); buffer.prevSequencedBlockNumber = uint64(block.number); }`
Alternatively, add a dedicated resetBufferAfterFork function callable when _chainIdChanged() is true
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy SequencerInbox with isDelayBufferable=true and valid buffer config. 2. Post several batches to set buffer.prevBlockNumber to a high value. 3. Simulate a chain fork by changing chainId and rolling back block.number below prevBlockNumber (using a custom test harness). 4. Call removeDelayAfterFork(). 5. Attempt to call addSequencerL2BatchFromOrigin with afterDelayedMessagesRead > totalDelayedMessagesRead. 6. Observe that the transaction reverts with arithmetic underflow.
uncertainties: Whether any real-world chain fork scenario actually results in block.number decreasing is uncertain - standard EVM forks continue block numbers
The exact behavior of Arbitrum L2/L3 chains during forks regarding block numbering is not fully documented in the provided code
Whether the admin recovery path via setBufferConfig works in all edge cases (specifically when bridge.delayedMessageCount() != totalDelayedMessagesRead)
additional_testing_needed: Test isSynced behavior when prevBlockNumber > block.number on a forked chain
Verify setBufferConfig recovery path works when buffer is in broken state
Test all batch posting variants to confirm which ones are affected
Test forceInclusion still works when isSynced would revert
Test on actual Arbitrum L2/L3 chains to determine block number behavior during forks
